q: In an AC circuit, the rms value of current is related to peak current (I₀) by:
exp: For sinusoidal AC, Irms = I₀/√2. RMS gives equivalent DC heating effect.

q: The phase difference between voltage and current in a pure inductor is:
exp: In inductors, current lags voltage by 90° due to back emf.

q: In a pure capacitor, current and voltage relation is:
exp: Capacitive current leads voltage because charge responds instantly to voltage change.

q: The inductive reactance XL is given by:
exp: XL = ωL, increases with frequency.

q: Capacitive reactance decreases when frequency increases because:
exp: Higher frequency allows easier charge flow through capacitor.

q: In a purely resistive AC circuit, phase difference between V and I is:
exp: Voltage and current are in phase for pure resistor.

q: The impedance of a series RLC circuit at resonance is:
exp: At resonance XL = XC, so impedance Z = R (minimum).

q: At resonance in series RLC circuit, current is:
exp: Impedance is minimum, so current is maximum.

q: Power factor in a purely inductive circuit is:
exp: Power factor = cos90° = 0.

q: Power factor in AC circuit is defined as:
opts: cos φ between V and I
exp: Power factor measures phase alignment of voltage and current.

q: Average power in AC circuit is given by:
exp: Pavg = Vrms Irms cosφ.

q: In ideal transformer, frequency of output is:
exp: Transformer does not change frequency.

q: Step-up transformer increases:
exp: Voltage increases while current decreases.

q: In AC generator, emf is maximum when coil plane is:
exp: Rate of change of flux is maximum when plane is parallel to field.

q: Angular frequency is related to frequency by:
exp: Standard relation in SHM and AC.

q: In LCR circuit at resonance, impedance is equal to:
exp: At resonance, inductive and capacitive reactances cancel.

q: Quality factor (Q) of a resonant circuit indicates:
exp: Higher Q means sharper resonance peak.

q: In AC circuit, energy is dissipated only in:
exp: Only resistors consume real power.

q: In a pure capacitor, average power over a cycle is:
exp: Energy stored is returned back in each cycle.

q: If frequency increases in AC circuit, inductive reactance:
exp: XL = ωL, so increases with frequency.

q: A device that converts AC to DC is:
exp: Rectifiers use diodes to convert AC into DC.

q: Power loss in transmission lines is minimized by:
exp: P loss = I²R, so reducing current reduces loss.

q: A transformer works on the principle of:
exp: Changing flux induces emf in secondary coil.

q: The phase difference between voltage and current in pure inductor is:
exp: Current lags voltage by 90°.

q: In AC circuit, impedance is analogous to:
exp: Impedance is total opposition to AC current.

q: Which component stores energy in electric field?
exp: Capacitor stores energy in electric field.

q: Which component stores energy in magnetic field?
exp: Inductor stores energy in magnetic field.

q: In a series RLC circuit, resonance occurs when:
exp: Reactances cancel each other at resonance.

q: At resonance, power factor is:
exp: Voltage and current are in phase at resonance.

q: In AC generator, induced emf depends on:
exp: ε = NBAω sinωt depends on all parameters.

q: A 50 Hz AC supply means:
exp: Frequency represents number of cycles per second.
